name: Khao-Tung Hna-Tung (Crusty Rice with Shrimp Dip).
recipeInstructions:
To prepare the coconut milk for the dip:
1. Mix 1 pound of grated, fresh coconut meat with 2-1/2 cups of hot (not boiling) water.
2. Stir well and strain out the liquid. Discard the coconut meat.
To make the basic "crusty rice":
1. Wash the rice if needed and place it in a pot or saucepan.
2. Add 6 cups of water and bring to a boil over medium heat until thoroughly cooked and most of the water has evaporated.
3. Remove from heat and let cool until it resembles a soggy mess.
4. Lightly coat a shallow tray or cookie sheet with oil, spread some of the cooled rice in a thin layer, and dry in the sun or warm oven.
5. Once dry but not fully crispy, cut into 2"-3" squares.
6. Dry thoroughly on the trays. Fry until lightly browned or toast until golden; drain well. Store in an airtight jar.
To prepare the shrimp dip:
1. In a saucepan, combine coconut milk with crushed cilantro roots/stems, chopped garlic, and ground raw shrimp meat.
2. Cook over medium heat until boiling, then add tomato paste and shallots (onions). Continue cooking until shallots are cooked through.
3. Add fish sauce, sugar, and crushed peanuts; bring to a second boil and remove from heat.
4. Serve warm in a bowl with toasted or fried crusty rice squares, garnished with chopped cilantro leaves and sliced fresh red chili peppers.
